scoped_claim: Agreement controller identification, notional basis, feature alignment, override pattern, and retrieval-attractor salience are separable agreement constructs.
collapsed_counterpart: Construction-specific agreement preference stores local agreement behaviour without controller/notional machinery.
discriminator: Low-frequency or novel QN-like animate plural frames selected before finite-agreement rows are inspected.
next_action: Run the AGR/List v2 denominator scout only after COCA authentication, then freeze finite-agreement follow-up strings.

scoped_claim: Pronoun/pro-form feature fit, reference tracking, personhood ascription, audience design, social-indexical value, and attribution are separable response channels.
collapsed_counterpart: Single audience-reaction baseline collapses reference success, acceptability, sanction, and attribution into one response tendency.
discriminator: Audience/reference pilot packet with separate response channels and hidden predictions.
next_action: Build the collapsed ART rival before interpreting any pilot-response summary as support for channel separation.

scoped_claim: Operator value, opportunity mass, recoverability, analogy, repair pressure, and context channels are separable in operator-gap cases.
collapsed_counterpart: Recoverability/listing baseline collapses opportunity, analogy, and licensing into observed availability plus semantic recoverability.
discriminator: Transparent-relative subtype lane and low-opportunity operator-gap cells where recoverability is high but licensing differs.
next_action: Build a minimal recoverability/listing rival and run it against the existing transparent-relative evaluation before adding more cards.

scoped_claim: Licensing, ideology, correction, production, acceptability, and attribution form a context/time-indexed feedback module.
collapsed_counterpart: Acceptability-normativity baseline collapses licensing, condemnation, and reported acceptability into one normative response variable.
discriminator: Frequent-condemned and robust-vernacular cells where production, licensing, and condemnation diverge.
next_action: Build a collapsed DYN rival and test whether it matches the existing context/dynamic evaluations without stratified nodes.

scoped_claim: Frame fit, repair-neighbour distance, and construction-specific dependency licensing are separable for fine-grained dependency/frame contrasts.
collapsed_counterpart: Stored-frame listing baseline treats each frame as a local preference without separate dependency-licensing or repair-neighbour constructs.
discriminator: Duration-have and island-construction variation cells, especially new frames not used to build FDL.
next_action: Build a stored-frame rival before giving FDL any projective credit.

scoped_claim: Processing cost, felt naturalness, recoverability, task framing, acceptability, and attribution are separable perturbation channels.
collapsed_counterpart: Processing-only baseline collapses naturalness, acceptability, and attribution into processing difficulty.
discriminator: Hard-but-licensed versus easy-but-unlicensed or masked-error contrasts with separated judgment channels.
next_action: Build a processing-only rival and compare on existing agreement-attraction, center-embedding, and ANN/brain measurement evaluations.

scoped_claim: Clause type, uptake role, repertoire closedness, token innovability, stance, repair, and attribution are separable at the operator/interjection boundary.
collapsed_counterpart: Public-consequence baseline treats any public social or interactional consequence as the same kind of grammar-relevant update.
discriminator: Clause-type/interjection and slur/register boundary cells where public consequence exists without closed operator licensing.
next_action: Build a public-consequence collapsed rival before strengthening UPT beyond scope-only.
